title: Le français en route vers le DELF

days.title: Le subjonctif présent : formation et déclencheurs
days.objective: Learn to form the present subjunctive and recognize the main triggers of obligation and will.
days.paretoFocus: Master 'il faut que' and 'vouloir que' + subjunctive, which cover the vast majority of everyday B1 uses.
drills: Conjugate five common verbs (parler, finir, prendre, faire, aller) in the present subjunctive out loud.
Transform ten 'il faut + infinitive' sentences into 'il faut que + subjunctive'.
Write three sentences with 'je veux que' about your daily routine.

items: Recall how the subjunctive stem is built from the third-person plural of the present tense.
Rewrite three indicative sentences as 'il faut que' + subjunctive.
List the irregular subjunctive forms of être, avoir, aller and faire.

activities.title: Former le subjonctif présent
activities.intro: The present subjunctive is built from the ils/elles present-tense stem plus the endings -e, -es, -e, -ions, -iez, -ent. It appears after triggers of obligation and will.

items.hr: Il faut que je parte tout de suite.
items.en: I have to leave right away.

items.hr: Il faut que tu fasses tes devoirs.
items.en: You have to do your homework.

items.hr: Je veux que nous soyons à l'heure.
items.en: I want us to be on time.

items.hr: Mes parents veulent que j'aille à l'université.
items.en: My parents want me to go to university.

items.hr: Il faut que vous preniez le train de huit heures.
items.en: You have to take the eight o'clock train.

activities.title: S'entraîner au subjonctif présent
activities.intro: Choose, complete or reorder to practise forming and triggering the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Il faut que tu ___ plus prudent.'
questions.explanation: After 'il faut que' the subjunctive is required; être → que tu sois.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Je veux que vous ___ la vérité.'
questions.explanation: 'vouloir que' triggers the subjunctive; dire → que vous disiez.

questions.prompt: Type the subjunctive of 'faire' for 'nous': 'Il faut que nous ___ attention.'
questions.explanation: faire has the irregular subjunctive stem 'fass-': que nous fassions.

questions.prompt: Type the subjunctive of 'avoir' for 'ils': 'Il faut qu'ils ___ leur passeport.'
questions.explanation: avoir is irregular in the subjunctive: que j'aie, que tu aies, qu'il ait, qu'ils aient.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I have to finish my work.'
questions.explanation: finir → que je finisse; the trigger 'il faut que' precedes the subjunctive clause.

activities.title: Chez le médecin
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You visit the doctor; practise reacting with 'il faut que' and 'je veux que'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonjour, qu'est-ce qui ne va pas aujourd'hui ?
lines.en: Hello, what's wrong today?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je suis très fatigué et j'ai mal à la gorge.
lines.en: I'm very tired and my throat hurts.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il faut que vous vous reposiez et que vous buviez beaucoup d'eau.
lines.en: You have to rest and drink a lot of water.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord. Faut-il que je prenne des médicaments ?
lines.en: All right. Do I have to take any medicine?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oui, je veux que vous preniez ce sirop deux fois par jour.
lines.en: Yes, I want you to take this syrup twice a day.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Très bien. Faut-il que je revienne la semaine prochaine ?
lines.en: Very well. Do I need to come back next week?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Seulement s'il faut que la fièvre continue.
lines.en: Only if the fever should continue.

days.title: Le subjonctif après les émotions et la nécessité
days.objective: Use the subjunctive after expressions of emotion, feeling and necessity.
days.paretoFocus: Learn a small set of high-frequency emotional triggers (content que, avoir peur que, dommage que) that unlock natural reactions.
drills: Complete ten sentences beginning with 'Je suis content(e) que...' about your friends.
Turn five 'C'est bien' statements into 'C'est bien que + subjunctive'.
Write three sentences with 'J'ai peur que' about the future.

items: Recall five emotional expressions that trigger the subjunctive.
Distinguish 'Je pense que + indicative' from 'Je ne pense pas que + subjunctive' with two examples.
Rewrite two 'il est important de + infinitive' sentences as 'il est important que + subjunctive'.

activities.title: Émotions, sentiments et nécessité
activities.intro: Verbs and expressions of emotion, judgement and necessity require the subjunctive in the subordinate clause when the subjects differ.

items.hr: Je suis content que tu sois là.
items.en: I'm glad you're here.

items.hr: C'est dommage que vous ne puissiez pas venir.
items.en: It's a shame you can't come.

items.hr: J'ai peur qu'il fasse froid demain.
items.en: I'm afraid it will be cold tomorrow.

items.hr: Il est important que nous sachions la vérité.
items.en: It's important that we know the truth.

items.hr: Je regrette que tu doives partir si tôt.
items.en: I'm sorry you have to leave so early.

activities.title: Réagir avec le subjonctif
activities.intro: Practise the subjunctive after emotion and necessity.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Je suis heureux que tu ___ réussi ton examen.'
questions.explanation: 'être heureux que' triggers the subjunctive; here the passé du subjonctif uses 'aies' + past participle.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'C'est dommage qu'il ___ malade.'
questions.explanation: 'C'est dommage que' expresses feeling and requires the subjunctive: qu'il soit.

questions.prompt: Type the subjunctive of 'pouvoir' for 'tu': 'Je suis triste que tu ne ___ pas rester.'
questions.explanation: pouvoir has the irregular subjunctive stem 'puiss-': que tu puisses.

questions.prompt: Type the subjunctive of 'savoir' for 'vous': 'Il faut que vous ___ nager.'
questions.explanation: savoir is irregular: que je sache, que vous sachiez.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I'm afraid it will rain tonight.'
questions.explanation: 'avoir peur que' triggers the subjunctive; pleuvoir → qu'il pleuve.

activities.title: Une nouvelle inattendue
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend shares news; react using emotions + subjunctive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai décidé de déménager à Lyon le mois prochain.
lines.en: I've decided to move to Lyon next month.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je suis content que tu aies trouvé un nouveau travail !
lines.en: I'm happy you've found a new job!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Merci, mais j'ai peur que ce soit difficile au début.
lines.en: Thanks, but I'm afraid it will be hard at first.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est normal que tu sois un peu inquiet.
lines.en: It's normal for you to be a little worried.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oui, et c'est dommage que nous soyons loin maintenant.
lines.en: Yes, and it's a shame we'll be far apart now.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il faut que nous nous appelions souvent, alors.
lines.en: Then we have to call each other often.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bien sûr ! Je veux que tu viennes me rendre visite.
lines.en: Of course! I want you to come visit me.

days.title: Le conditionnel présent : politesse et hypothèse
days.objective: Form the present conditional and use it for polite requests and hypothetical situations.
days.paretoFocus: Master 'je voudrais', 'pourriez-vous' and 'j'aimerais', the phrases that make requests sound polite at B1.
drills: Rewrite five direct requests ('Je veux...') as polite conditional forms ('Je voudrais...').
Conjugate aller, faire, venir and pouvoir in the present conditional.
Write three 'À ta place, je...' sentences giving advice.

items: Recall that the conditional uses the future stem plus the imparfait endings.
Turn three commands into polite requests with 'pourriez-vous'.
Give two pieces of advice using 'tu devrais' + infinitive.

activities.title: Le conditionnel de politesse
activities.intro: The conditional is formed with the future stem + the imperfect endings (-ais, -ais, -ait, -ions, -iez, -aient). It softens requests and expresses wishes.

items.hr: Je voudrais un café, s'il vous plaît.
items.en: I would like a coffee, please.

items.hr: Pourriez-vous m'aider, s'il vous plaît ?
items.en: Could you help me, please?

items.hr: J'aimerais visiter le musée demain.
items.en: I would like to visit the museum tomorrow.

items.hr: Tu devrais te reposer un peu.
items.en: You should rest a little.

items.hr: Nous serions ravis de vous accueillir.
items.en: We would be delighted to welcome you.

activities.title: Demander poliment
activities.intro: Practise forming and using the present conditional.

questions.prompt: Choose the polite form: '___ me dire où est la gare ?'
questions.explanation: The conditional 'pourriez-vous' is the most polite way to ask; 'pouvez-vous' is more direct.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'À ta place, je ___ un médecin.'
questions.explanation: Advice with 'à ta place' uses the conditional: je consulterais.

questions.prompt: Type the conditional of 'aimer' for 'je': 'J'___ voyager en Italie.'
questions.explanation: The conditional of aimer for 'je' is 'aimerais' (future stem aimer- + -ais).

questions.prompt: Type the conditional of 'faire' for 'nous': 'Nous ___ un long voyage si nous avions le temps.'
questions.explanation: faire has the irregular future/conditional stem 'fer-': nous ferions.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I would like to book a table for two.'
questions.explanation: 'Je voudrais' + infinitive is the standard polite way to make a request.

activities.title: Au restaurant
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Order politely at a restaurant using the conditional.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonsoir, vous avez choisi ?
lines.en: Good evening, have you chosen?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bonsoir. Je voudrais le menu du jour, s'il vous plaît.
lines.en: Good evening. I would like the set menu, please.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Très bien. Et comme boisson ?
lines.en: Very well. And to drink?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pourriez-vous me conseiller un vin léger ?
lines.en: Could you recommend a light wine to me?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bien sûr, je vous recommanderais ce rosé.
lines.en: Of course, I would recommend this rosé.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait. J'aimerais aussi une carafe d'eau, s'il vous plaît.
lines.en: Perfect. I would also like a carafe of water, please.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je vous apporte tout ça tout de suite.
lines.en: I'll bring you all that right away.

days.title: Les phrases avec « si » : si + imparfait, conditionnel
days.objective: Build hypothetical sentences with 'si' + imperfect in the main-clause conditional.
days.paretoFocus: Lock in the pattern 'Si + imparfait, ... conditionnel présent' for talking about unreal or imagined situations.
drills: Complete five 'Si j'avais..., je...' sentences about money and travel.
Match ten 'si' clauses with the correct conditional main clauses.
Write three sentences about what you would do with more free time.

items: Recall the tense pairing: si + imparfait → conditionnel présent.
Fix three sentences that wrongly use the conditional after 'si'.
Write two hypotheses about your ideal city.

activities.title: L'hypothèse irréelle du présent
activities.intro: To express an unreal or imagined present situation, use 'si' + imperfect in the condition and the present conditional in the result. Never use the conditional directly after 'si'.

items.hr: Si j'avais plus de temps, je voyagerais davantage.
items.en: If I had more time, I would travel more.

items.hr: Si nous habitions à la campagne, nous serions plus tranquilles.
items.en: If we lived in the countryside, we would be calmer.

items.hr: Qu'est-ce que tu ferais si tu gagnais au loto ?
items.en: What would you do if you won the lottery?

items.hr: Si elle parlait mieux anglais, elle trouverait ce travail.
items.en: If she spoke better English, she would find that job.

items.hr: Je t'aiderais si tu me le demandais.
items.en: I would help you if you asked me.

activities.title: Construire des hypothèses
activities.intro: Practise the si + imparfait / conditionnel pattern.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Si j'___ riche, j'achèterais une maison.'
questions.explanation: After 'si' in an unreal hypothesis, use the imperfect: si j'étais riche.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Si nous avions une voiture, nous ___ à la mer.'
questions.explanation: The result clause uses the present conditional: nous irions (aller → ir-).

questions.prompt: Type the conditional of 'être' for 'tu': 'Si tu dormais plus, tu ___ moins fatigué.'
questions.explanation: si + imparfait (dormais) pairs with the conditional: tu serais.

questions.prompt: Type the imperfect of 'avoir' for 'je': 'Si j'___ le choix, je resterais ici.'
questions.explanation: The 'si' clause takes the imperfect: si j'avais le choix.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'If you came, we would be glad.'
questions.explanation: si + imparfait (venais) in the condition, conditionnel (serions) in the result.

activities.title: Et si on rêvait un peu ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Imagine a different life with a friend using 'si' + imperfect.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce que tu ferais si tu pouvais changer de vie ?
lines.en: What would you do if you could change your life?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si j'avais le courage, je partirais vivre à l'étranger.
lines.en: If I had the courage, I would go live abroad.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ah oui ? Et où est-ce que tu irais ?
lines.en: Oh really? And where would you go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si je parlais mieux espagnol, je m'installerais à Séville.
lines.en: If I spoke better Spanish, I would settle in Seville.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ce serait magnifique. Moi, si je pouvais, j'ouvrirais un café.
lines.en: That would be wonderful. Me, if I could, I would open a café.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si tu ouvrais un café, je viendrais tous les jours !
lines.en: If you opened a café, I would come every day!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors il faudrait vraiment qu'on réalise ces rêves.
lines.en: Then we would really have to make these dreams come true.

days.title: Les pronoms relatifs « dont » et « où » + ce qui / ce que
days.objective: Connect clauses with the relative pronouns dont and où and the indefinite relatives ce qui and ce que.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'dont' for de-complements and 'ce que/ce qui' to report vague ideas, both frequent in B1 speech.
drills: Combine ten sentence pairs using 'qui', 'que', 'dont' or 'où'.
Rewrite five sentences replacing 'la chose que' with 'ce que'.
Write three sentences describing a place using 'où'.

items: Recall when to use 'dont' (verbs/expressions with de).
Distinguish 'ce qui' (subject) from 'ce que' (object) with two examples.
Combine two clauses with 'où' referring to time and place.

activities.title: Relier des idées avec dont et où
activities.intro: 'dont' replaces a complement introduced by 'de'; 'où' refers to place or time; 'ce qui'/'ce que' refer to an unnamed idea (subject vs object).

items.hr: C'est le livre dont je t'ai parlé.
items.en: This is the book I told you about.

items.hr: Voici la ville où je suis né.
items.en: Here is the town where I was born.

items.hr: Le jour où nous nous sommes rencontrés était pluvieux.
items.en: The day we met was rainy.

items.hr: Je ne comprends pas ce qui se passe.
items.en: I don't understand what is happening.

items.hr: Dis-moi ce que tu veux.
items.en: Tell me what you want.

activities.title: Choisir le bon pronom relatif
activities.intro: Practise dont, où, ce qui and ce que.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct pronoun: 'C'est l'ami ___ j'ai besoin.'
questions.explanation: 'avoir besoin de' takes 'de', so the relative pronoun is 'dont'.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct pronoun: 'Je ne sais pas ___ tu penses.'
questions.explanation: 'penser' here has a direct object idea, so the object form 'ce que' is used.

questions.prompt: Type the relative pronoun (place): 'Voilà le restaurant ___ nous avons dîné.'
questions.explanation: 'où' refers to the place where the action happens.

questions.prompt: Type the indefinite relative (subject): '___ m'inquiète, c'est le prix.'
questions.explanation: The verb 'inquiète' needs a subject, so use 'ce qui'.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'It's the film everyone is talking about.'
questions.explanation: 'parler de' → dont; the relative clause describes 'le film'.

activities.title: Parler d'un voyage
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Describe a trip using relative pronouns.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu m'avais parlé d'un endroit magnifique, c'était où ?
lines.en: You'd told me about a wonderful place, where was it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est un petit village où j'ai passé mon enfance.
lines.en: It's a little village where I spent my childhood.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et qu'est-ce qui t'a le plus marqué là-bas ?
lines.en: And what struck you the most there?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ce que j'ai préféré, c'était les paysages près de la rivière.
lines.en: What I liked best was the scenery near the river.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est le voyage dont tu rêvais depuis longtemps, non ?
lines.en: It's the trip you'd been dreaming of for a long time, right?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Exactement. C'est un souvenir dont je suis très fier.
lines.en: Exactly. It's a memory I'm very proud of.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'aimerais voir les photos de ce que tu décris.
lines.en: I'd love to see the photos of what you're describing.

days.title: Le plus-que-parfait
days.objective: Form and use the pluperfect to express an action completed before another past action.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'avait/était + past participle' to make the sequence of past events clear when telling stories.
drills: Conjugate five verbs in the plus-que-parfait, choosing avoir or être correctly.
Complete ten sentences with 'quand je suis arrivé, il avait déjà...'.
Rewrite three short stories adding an earlier action in the pluperfect.

items: Recall the pluperfect formula: imparfait of avoir/être + past participle.
Order two past events correctly using passé composé and plus-que-parfait.
Check past-participle agreement in three être sentences.

activities.title: Raconter un passé antérieur à un autre
activities.intro: The pluperfect uses the imperfect of avoir or être plus the past participle. It marks an action that happened before another past action.

items.hr: Quand je suis arrivé, le train était déjà parti.
items.en: When I arrived, the train had already left.

items.hr: Elle avait fini ses devoirs avant de sortir.
items.en: She had finished her homework before going out.

items.hr: Nous n'avions jamais vu un tel spectacle.
items.en: We had never seen such a show.

items.hr: Ils s'étaient couchés tôt ce soir-là.
items.en: They had gone to bed early that evening.

items.hr: J'ai compris qu'elle m'avait menti.
items.en: I understood that she had lied to me.

activities.title: Situer les actions dans le temps
activities.intro: Practise forming the pluperfect and ordering past events.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Quand nous sommes entrés, le film ___ déjà commencé.'
questions.explanation: commencer takes avoir here; the pluperfect uses the imperfect of avoir: avait commencé.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Elle ___ partie avant mon arrivée.'
questions.explanation: partir uses être, so the pluperfect is 'était partie' with agreement.

questions.prompt: Type the pluperfect of 'finir' for 'je' (avoir): 'J'___ ___ quand tu as appelé.'
questions.explanation: Pluperfect = imperfect of avoir (avais) + past participle (fini).

questions.prompt: Type the pluperfect of 'aller' for 'ils' (être): 'Ils ___ ___ au marché avant midi.'
questions.explanation: aller uses être; the participle agrees: étaient allés.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'He had forgotten his umbrella that day.'
questions.explanation: Pluperfect: avait (imperfect of avoir) + oublié (past participle).

activities.title: Un rendez-vous manqué
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Explain a missed meeting using the pluperfect.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je t'ai attendu au café, mais tu n'es pas venu.
lines.en: I waited for you at the café, but you didn't come.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Désolé, quand je suis arrivé, tu étais déjà parti.
lines.en: Sorry, when I arrived, you had already left.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ah bon ? Je n'avais pas reçu ton message.
lines.en: Really? I hadn't received your message.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je te l'avais envoyé, mais mon téléphone s'était éteint.
lines.en: I had sent it to you, but my phone had turned off.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ce n'est pas grave. On avait mal choisi l'heure, c'est tout.
lines.en: It doesn't matter. We had chosen the wrong time, that's all.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est vrai. Je n'avais pas pensé à la circulation.
lines.en: That's true. I hadn't thought about the traffic.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On se rattrapera demain, d'accord ?
lines.en: We'll make up for it tomorrow, okay?

days.title: Le discours indirect au présent
days.objective: Report statements, questions and orders in indirect speech in the present.
days.paretoFocus: Master the connectors que, si, ce que and de + infinitive that convert direct speech into reported speech.
drills: Transform five direct statements into 'Il dit que...'.
Turn five yes/no questions into 'Il demande si...'.
Convert three orders into 'Elle me dit de + infinitive'.

items: Recall the pronoun and possessive changes from direct to indirect speech.
Report two questions, one with 'si' and one with 'ce que'.
Report one order using 'demander de + infinitive'.

activities.title: Rapporter les paroles des autres
activities.intro: In present indirect speech, statements use 'que', yes/no questions use 'si', 'qu'est-ce que' becomes 'ce que', and orders use 'de' + infinitive. Pronouns and possessives adapt.

items.hr: Il dit qu'il est fatigué.
items.en: He says he is tired.

items.hr: Elle demande si tu viens ce soir.
items.en: She asks whether you're coming tonight.

items.hr: Je me demande ce que tu veux dire.
items.en: I wonder what you mean.

items.hr: Le professeur nous demande de nous taire.
items.en: The teacher asks us to be quiet.

items.hr: Marie dit qu'elle adore son nouveau travail.
items.en: Marie says she loves her new job.

activities.title: Transformer le discours direct
activities.intro: Practise reporting speech in the present.

questions.prompt: Report: Il dit : « Je suis prêt. » → 'Il dit ___ prêt.'
questions.explanation: A statement is reported with 'que' and the pronoun changes from 'je' to 'il'.

questions.prompt: Report: Elle demande : « Tu as faim ? » → 'Elle demande ___ faim.'
questions.explanation: A yes/no question is reported with 'si'.

questions.prompt: Report the order: Il me dit : « Attends ! » → 'Il me dit ___ attendre.'
questions.explanation: Orders are reported with 'de' + infinitive; before a vowel it elides to d'attendre.

questions.prompt: Report: Elle demande : « Que fais-tu ? » → 'Elle demande ___ tu fais.'
questions.explanation: A direct question with 'que' ('Que fais-tu ?' / 'Qu'est-ce que tu fais ?') becomes 'ce que' in indirect speech.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'He asks if we are free tomorrow.'
questions.explanation: A yes/no question is reported with 'si' + indicative clause.

activities.title: Passer un message
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Relay what other people said using indirect speech.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce que Paul a dit au téléphone ?
lines.en: What did Paul say on the phone?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il dit qu'il ne peut pas venir à la réunion.
lines.en: He says he can't come to the meeting.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et il demande quelque chose ?
lines.en: And is he asking for anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, il demande si tu peux le rappeler ce soir.
lines.en: Yes, he's asking whether you can call him back tonight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: D'accord. Et Sophie, elle veut quoi ?
lines.en: Okay. And Sophie, what does she want?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Elle me demande de te dire de préparer les documents.
lines.en: She's asking me to tell you to prepare the documents.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Très bien, dis-lui que tout sera prêt.
lines.en: Very well, tell her everything will be ready.

days.title: Le gérondif (en + -ant) et le passif
days.objective: Use the gerund to express simultaneity or manner and form the passive voice.
days.paretoFocus: Combine 'en + present participle' for 'while/by doing' and 'être + past participle + par' for the passive.
drills: Form the gerund of five verbs and use each in a sentence about your morning.
Rewrite five active sentences in the passive with 'par'.
Write three sentences describing how you learn using 'en'.

items: Recall that the gerund is formed from the nous-form stem + -ant, preceded by 'en'.
Turn two active sentences into passive ones with agreement.
Explain the difference between manner and simultaneity with 'en'.

activities.title: Le gérondif et la voix passive
activities.intro: The gerund ('en' + present participle) expresses simultaneity, manner or means. The passive uses 'être' + past participle (+ 'par' + agent), with the participle agreeing with the subject.

items.hr: Il écoute de la musique en travaillant.
items.en: He listens to music while working.

items.hr: En prenant le métro, tu arriveras plus vite.
items.en: By taking the metro, you'll arrive faster.

items.hr: Ce roman a été écrit par un jeune auteur.
items.en: This novel was written by a young author.

items.hr: La lettre est envoyée chaque matin.
items.en: The letter is sent every morning.

items.hr: Les fenêtres ont été fermées à cause du vent.
items.en: The windows were closed because of the wind.

activities.title: Gérondif et passif en pratique
activities.intro: Practise forming gerunds and passive sentences.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct gerund: 'Elle chante ___ la douche.'
questions.explanation: The gerund needs 'en' + present participle: en prenant.

questions.prompt: Choose the passive form: 'Le voleur ___ par la police.'
questions.explanation: The passive uses être (here in the passé composé: a été) + past participle + par.

questions.prompt: Type the gerund of 'finir': 'Il a réussi ___ ___ ses devoirs à temps.'
questions.explanation: The gerund is 'en' + present participle; finir → finissant, so 'en finissant'.

questions.prompt: Complete the passive: 'Ces tableaux ___ ___ ___ par Monet.' (peindre, passé composé)
questions.explanation: Passive passé composé: ont été + past participle 'peints', agreeing with the masculine plural subject.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'He hurt himself playing football.'
questions.explanation: The gerund 'en jouant' expresses the circumstance in which the action happened.

activities.title: Visite d'un musée
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Talk about a museum using gerunds and the passive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment as-tu appris autant sur ce peintre ?
lines.en: How did you learn so much about this painter?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai beaucoup appris en lisant le guide du musée.
lines.en: I learned a lot by reading the museum guide.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ce tableau est magnifique. Il a été peint quand ?
lines.en: This painting is magnificent. When was it painted?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il a été peint au dix-neuvième siècle par un artiste célèbre.
lines.en: It was painted in the nineteenth century by a famous artist.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On peut prendre des photos ?
lines.en: Can we take photos?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, mais en éteignant le flash, s'il te plaît.
lines.en: Yes, but by turning off the flash, please.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: D'accord. Ces œuvres sont vraiment bien protégées.
lines.en: Okay. These works are really well protected.

days.title: Donner et justifier son opinion
days.objective: Learn to express and defend a personal opinion using nuanced phrases and the subjunctive after doubt or feeling.
days.paretoFocus: Master 'à mon avis', 'je trouve que', and 'je ne pense pas que + subjonctif' to open almost any B1 argument.
drills: Write five opinions on random topics, each starting with a different phrase (à mon avis, selon moi, je trouve que, il me semble que, personnellement).
Transform five affirmative opinions into negative ones using 'je ne pense pas que' + subjonctif.
Record yourself defending an opinion for one minute using at least two justifications ('parce que', 'en effet').

items: Recall the difference between 'je pense que + indicatif' and 'je ne pense pas que + subjonctif'.
Say aloud three phrases to introduce an opinion.
Give one opinion and immediately justify it with 'parce que'.

activities.title: Exprimer et justifier une opinion
activities.intro: Key phrases to state, nuance, and justify an opinion at B1 level.

items.hr: À mon avis, ce film est excellent.
items.en: In my opinion, this film is excellent.

items.hr: Je trouve que cette idée est intéressante.
items.en: I think that this idea is interesting.

items.hr: Je ne pense pas que ce soit une bonne solution.
items.en: I don't think that's a good solution.

items.hr: Selon moi, il faut agir rapidement.
items.en: According to me, we must act quickly.

items.hr: Il me semble que tu as raison.
items.en: It seems to me that you are right.

items.hr: Je suis convaincu que nous pouvons réussir.
items.en: I am convinced that we can succeed.

activities.title: Opinion et subjonctif
activities.intro: Practise opinion phrases and the subjunctive after negated belief.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the subjunctive after a negated opinion?
options: Je ne pense pas qu'il vient.
Je ne pense pas qu'il vienne.
Je ne pense pas qu'il viendra.
Je ne pense pas qu'il venait.
questions.answer: Je ne pense pas qu'il vienne.
questions.explanation: 'Je ne pense pas que' expresses doubt, so it requires the subjunctive 'vienne'.

questions.prompt: Choose the phrase that introduces an opinion followed by the indicative.
questions.explanation: 'Je trouve que' expresses a personal judgement and is followed by the indicative; the others require the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the subjunctive of 'être': 'Je ne crois pas que ce ___ juste.'
questions.explanation: After 'je ne crois pas que', the verb 'être' becomes the subjunctive 'soit'.

questions.prompt: Type a phrase meaning 'In my opinion' to start a sentence.
questions.explanation: 'À mon avis' is a standard B1 way to introduce a personal opinion.

questions.prompt: Which sentence justifies an opinion?
options: Je pense que c'est utile parce que ça fait gagner du temps.
questions.answer: Je pense que c'est utile parce que ça fait gagner du temps.
questions.explanation: It states an opinion and gives a reason with 'parce que'.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I don't think that's true.'
questions.explanation: The negated opinion 'je ne pense pas que' is followed by the subjunctive 'soit'.

activities.title: Un débat entre amis
activities.intro: You play 'Me', sharing and defending an opinion about city life.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu préfères vivre en ville ou à la campagne ?
lines.en: Do you prefer living in the city or the countryside?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: À mon avis, la ville est plus pratique.
lines.en: In my opinion, the city is more practical.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vraiment ? Pourquoi penses-tu ça ?
lines.en: Really? Why do you think that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je trouve que les transports sont meilleurs et qu'il y a plus de travail.
lines.en: I think the transport is better and that there is more work.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mais la campagne est plus calme, non ?
lines.en: But the countryside is calmer, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est vrai, mais je ne pense pas que ce soit plus intéressant.
lines.en: That's true, but I don't think it's more interesting.

days.title: La société et les problèmes actuels
days.objective: Acquire vocabulary and structures to discuss social issues and current problems.
days.paretoFocus: Learn core social-issue nouns and cause-effect connectors to describe and analyse problems.
drills: List six current social problems in French with a short definition each.
Write three sentences linking a problem to a cause using 'à cause de' or 'en raison de'.
Propose one solution for each of three problems using 'il faudrait' + infinitive.

items: Recall five social-issue nouns (le chômage, la pauvreté, ...).
Make a sentence with 'à cause de' expressing a cause.
Suggest a solution with 'il faudrait'.

activities.title: Le vocabulaire des problèmes de société
activities.intro: Essential nouns and expressions to talk about society and its challenges.

items.hr: le chômage
items.en: unemployment
items.note: un problème majeur pour les jeunes

items.hr: la pauvreté
items.en: poverty

items.hr: les inégalités sociales
items.en: social inequalities

items.hr: la discrimination
items.en: discrimination

items.hr: l'exclusion (f.)
items.en: exclusion

items.hr: Il faudrait lutter contre la pauvreté.
items.en: We should fight against poverty.
items.note: 'il faudrait' + infinitive to propose a solution

activities.title: Parler des problèmes de société
activities.intro: Test your social vocabulary and cause-effect structures.

questions.prompt: Which word means 'unemployment'?
questions.explanation: 'Le chômage' is the state of being without work.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct connector to express a cause: 'Il y a de la pauvreté ___ chômage.'
questions.explanation: 'À cause de' introduces a negative cause; before 'le chômage' it contracts to 'à cause du'.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Il faudrait ___ (lutter) contre les inégalités.'
questions.explanation: 'Il faudrait' is followed by the infinitive 'lutter'.

questions.prompt: Type the French word for 'social inequalities' (plural).
questions.explanation: 'Les inégalités sociales' is a common B1 phrase for social differences.

questions.prompt: Which sentence proposes a solution?
questions.explanation: 'Il faudrait créer' proposes an action to solve a problem.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Poverty is rising because of unemployment.'
questions.explanation: 'À cause du' introduces the cause, here 'le chômage'.

activities.title: Discussion sur la société
activities.intro: You play 'Me', discussing a social problem with a friend.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quel est le plus grand problème de notre société selon toi ?
lines.en: What is the biggest problem of our society, in your view?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je pense que le chômage des jeunes est très inquiétant.
lines.en: I think youth unemployment is very worrying.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourquoi est-ce si grave ?
lines.en: Why is it so serious?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parce que sans travail, beaucoup de jeunes tombent dans la pauvreté.
lines.en: Because without work, many young people fall into poverty.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que pourrait-on faire ?
lines.en: What could we do?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il faudrait investir dans la formation et créer plus d'emplois.
lines.en: We should invest in training and create more jobs.

days.title: Les médias et l'information
days.objective: Build vocabulary and expressions to talk about media, news, and reliability of information.
days.paretoFocus: Master media nouns and verbs of reporting to discuss where and how you get information.
drills: Name five media sources in French and say how often you use each.
Write two sentences distinguishing 'une information fiable' from 'une fausse information'.
Report a piece of news using 'd'après' or 'selon' + source.

items: Recall five media-related nouns (la presse, la chaîne, ...).
Use 'selon' to attribute information to a source.
Explain 'une fausse information' in one French sentence.

activities.title: Le vocabulaire des médias
activities.intro: Words to describe media, journalism, and the reliability of information.

items.hr: la presse écrite
items.en: the written press

items.hr: une chaîne de télévision
items.en: a television channel

items.hr: un réseau social
items.en: a social network

items.hr: une information fiable
items.en: reliable information

items.hr: une fausse information
items.en: fake news / false information

items.hr: D'après le journal, le prix va augmenter.
items.en: According to the newspaper, the price will rise.

activities.title: S'informer avec les médias
activities.intro: Check your media vocabulary and how to cite a source.

questions.prompt: Which phrase means 'a social network'?
questions.explanation: 'Un réseau social' translates 'a social network', e.g. Instagram.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct way to attribute information: '___ la radio, il va pleuvoir.'
questions.explanation: 'Selon' means 'according to' and attributes information to a source.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'reliable information'.
questions.explanation: 'Fiable' means trustworthy or reliable.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Il faut vérifier ses sources pour éviter les ___ informations.'
questions.explanation: 'Fausses informations' (fake news) is the plural of 'fausse information'.

questions.prompt: Which sentence expresses distrust of a source?
options: Je ne fais pas confiance à ce site.
questions.answer: Je ne fais pas confiance à ce site.
questions.explanation: 'Ne pas faire confiance à' expresses distrust of a source.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'According to the newspaper, the situation will change.'
questions.explanation: 'Selon le journal' attributes the information to the newspaper.

activities.title: Où t'informes-tu ?
activities.intro: You play 'Me', talking about how you get your news.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment est-ce que tu t'informes en général ?
lines.en: How do you usually get your information?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je lis surtout la presse en ligne et j'écoute la radio.
lines.en: I mostly read the online press and listen to the radio.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et les réseaux sociaux, tu leur fais confiance ?
lines.en: And social networks, do you trust them?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pas vraiment, car il y a beaucoup de fausses informations.
lines.en: Not really, because there is a lot of fake news.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment fais-tu pour vérifier ?
lines.en: How do you check?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je compare plusieurs sources fiables avant de croire une nouvelle.
lines.en: I compare several reliable sources before believing a piece of news.

days.title: Le travail et les études
days.objective: Describe your studies, career path, and professional projects using future and past structures.
days.paretoFocus: Learn vocabulary for education and work plus 'j'aimerais' / 'je voudrais' to talk about projects.
drills: Describe your own study or career path in four sentences using the passé composé.
Write three professional goals using 'j'aimerais' or 'je voudrais' + infinitive.
List five job-related nouns with their gender (un emploi, une entreprise, ...).

items: Recall five work/study nouns (un stage, un entretien, ...).
State a career goal with 'j'aimerais'.
Say one thing you studied using the passé composé.

activities.title: Parcours et projets professionnels
activities.intro: Vocabulary and phrases to talk about studies, jobs, and ambitions.

items.hr: un stage
items.en: an internship

items.hr: un entretien d'embauche
items.en: a job interview

items.hr: une entreprise
items.en: a company

items.hr: J'ai fait des études de droit.
items.en: I studied law.
items.note: passé composé to describe a completed course of study

items.hr: J'aimerais travailler à l'étranger.
items.en: I would like to work abroad.

items.hr: Je voudrais changer de métier.
items.en: I would like to change careers.

activities.title: Le monde du travail
activities.intro: Practise work vocabulary and expressing professional plans.

questions.prompt: Which word means 'an internship'?
questions.explanation: 'Un stage' is a period of practical training in a company.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct phrase to express a career wish.
questions.explanation: 'J'aimerais' + infinitive politely expresses a wish or project.

questions.prompt: Complete in passé composé: 'J'___ (faire) un stage l'été dernier.'
questions.explanation: 'Faire' in the passé composé with 'j'' is 'j'ai fait'.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'a job interview'.
questions.explanation: 'Un entretien d'embauche' is the meeting to get hired.

questions.prompt: Which sentence describes a completed part of your studies?
questions.explanation: The passé composé 'j'ai étudié' describes a completed study.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I would like to work in a big company.'
questions.explanation: 'J'aimerais' + infinitive expresses a professional wish.

activities.title: Un entretien informel
activities.intro: You play 'Me', talking about your studies and career plans.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce que tu as étudié ?
lines.en: What did you study?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai fait des études de commerce et j'ai fait un stage dans une banque.
lines.en: I studied business and did an internship in a bank.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et maintenant, quels sont tes projets ?
lines.en: And now, what are your plans?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'aimerais trouver un poste dans le marketing.
lines.en: I would like to find a position in marketing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu voudrais rester ici ou partir à l'étranger ?
lines.en: Would you like to stay here or go abroad?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je voudrais travailler à l'étranger pour améliorer mes langues.
lines.en: I would like to work abroad to improve my languages.

days.title: L'environnement et l'écologie
days.objective: Discuss environmental issues and eco-friendly actions with relevant vocabulary.
days.paretoFocus: Learn environment nouns and 'il faut' / gérondif to express what should be done to protect the planet.
drills: List six ecological problems or actions in French (le réchauffement climatique, le recyclage, ...).
Write three eco-gestures using 'il faut' + infinitive.
Describe one daily habit that protects the environment using 'en' + gérondif.

items: Recall five environment nouns (la pollution, le tri, ...).
Make an eco-recommendation with 'il faut'.
Use the gérondif to link two actions ('en triant...').

activities.title: Protéger l'environnement
activities.intro: Key vocabulary and structures for ecology and green actions.

items.hr: le réchauffement climatique
items.en: global warming

items.hr: la pollution
items.en: pollution

items.hr: le recyclage
items.en: recycling

items.hr: trier les déchets
items.en: to sort waste

items.hr: Il faut réduire notre consommation d'énergie.
items.en: We must reduce our energy consumption.

items.hr: On protège la planète en triant les déchets.
items.en: We protect the planet by sorting waste.

activities.title: Gestes pour la planète
activities.intro: Test environmental vocabulary and the gérondif.

questions.prompt: Which phrase means 'global warming'?
questions.explanation: 'Le réchauffement climatique' is the rise in global temperatures.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct gérondif: 'On économise l'eau ___ le robinet.'
questions.explanation: 'En' + present participle ('fermant') forms the gérondif expressing means.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Il faut ___ (trier) les déchets.'
questions.explanation: 'Il faut' is followed by the infinitive 'trier'.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'pollution'.
questions.explanation: 'La pollution' is the contamination of the environment.

questions.prompt: Which action helps the environment?
questions.explanation: Using public transport reduces pollution.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'We must protect the planet by recycling.'
questions.explanation: 'Il faut' + infinitive gives advice, and 'en recyclant' (gérondif) shows how.

activities.title: Discussion écologique
activities.intro: You play 'Me', talking about green habits with a friend.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que fais-tu pour protéger l'environnement ?
lines.en: What do you do to protect the environment?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je trie mes déchets et j'utilise le vélo pour aller au travail.
lines.en: I sort my waste and use my bike to go to work.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est bien ! Et pour l'énergie ?
lines.en: That's good! And for energy?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'économise l'électricité en éteignant les lumières.
lines.en: I save electricity by turning off the lights.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Penses-tu que ça suffit ?
lines.en: Do you think that's enough?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, je pense qu'il faut aussi consommer moins et recycler davantage.
lines.en: No, I think we must also consume less and recycle more.

days.title: Raconter une expérience personnelle
days.objective: Narrate a past personal experience combining passé composé and imparfait correctly.
days.paretoFocus: Master the contrast between passé composé (events) and imparfait (background) to tell a story.
drills: Write a short story (five sentences) about a trip, mixing passé composé and imparfait.
Identify in three sentences which verb is the background (imparfait) and which is the event (passé composé).
Add time connectors ('d'abord', 'ensuite', 'enfin') to sequence a past narrative.

items: Recall when to use imparfait vs passé composé.
Give three sequencing connectors for a story.
Tell one past event about your day using the passé composé.

activities.title: Le récit au passé
activities.intro: How to combine tenses and connectors to tell a personal story.

items.hr: Quand j'étais jeune, j'habitais à Lyon.
items.en: When I was young, I lived in Lyon.

items.hr: Hier, j'ai rencontré un vieil ami.
items.en: Yesterday, I met an old friend.
items.note: passé composé for a specific event

items.hr: Il faisait beau quand nous sommes partis.
items.en: The weather was nice when we left.

items.hr: D'abord, nous avons visité le musée.
items.en: First, we visited the museum.

items.hr: Ensuite, nous avons mangé au restaurant.
items.en: Then, we ate at the restaurant.

items.hr: Enfin, nous sommes rentrés à la maison.
items.en: Finally, we went back home.

activities.title: Passé composé ou imparfait ?
activities.intro: Practise choosing the right past tense and connectors.

questions.prompt: Which tense describes a background situation in the past?
questions.explanation: The imparfait describes ongoing background or habitual actions in the past.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence combining both past tenses.
options: Je dormais quand le téléphone a sonné.
Je dormais quand le téléphone sonnait.
J'ai dormi quand le téléphone a sonné.
Je dors quand le téléphone a sonné.
questions.answer: Je dormais quand le téléphone a sonné.
questions.explanation: The imparfait 'dormais' sets the scene; the passé composé 'a sonné' marks the sudden event.

questions.prompt: Complete in passé composé: 'Hier, nous ___ (aller) au cinéma.'
questions.explanation: 'Aller' uses 'être' in the passé composé: 'nous sommes allés'.

questions.prompt: Type the connector meaning 'then / next' used to sequence a story.
questions.explanation: 'Ensuite' links the next step of a narrative.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the imparfait for a repeated past habit?
options: Je suis allé à Paris une fois.
Tous les étés, j'allais à la mer.
questions.answer: Tous les étés, j'allais à la mer.
questions.explanation: 'Tous les étés, j'allais' shows a repeated habit, so the imparfait is used.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'When he arrived, I was watching television.'
questions.explanation: The event 'est arrivé' (passé composé) interrupts the background 'regardais' (imparfait).

activities.title: Raconte-moi tes vacances
activities.intro: You play 'Me', narrating a past holiday experience.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment se sont passées tes vacances ?
lines.en: How were your holidays?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'était génial ! Il faisait beau tous les jours.
lines.en: It was great! The weather was nice every day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce que tu as fait ?
lines.en: What did you do?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'abord, j'ai visité la vieille ville, puis j'ai nagé dans la mer.
lines.en: First, I visited the old town, then I swam in the sea.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu étais seul ?
lines.en: Were you alone?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, j'étais avec des amis et nous avons beaucoup ri.
lines.en: No, I was with friends and we laughed a lot.

days.title: Écrire une lettre ou un mél
days.objective: Write a formal or informal letter and email choosing the appropriate register.
days.paretoFocus: Learn opening and closing formulas and register markers to structure any B1 letter or email.
drills: Write a formal opening and closing for an email to a company.
Rewrite an informal message into a formal one, changing 'tu' to 'vous'.
Draft a five-line email requesting information using polite formulas.

items: Recall two formal and two informal closing formulas.
Choose 'tu' or 'vous' for three situations.
Write one polite request with 'Je vous serais reconnaissant de'.

activities.title: Le registre dans la correspondance
activities.intro: Opening, closing, and register formulas for letters and emails.

items.hr: Madame, Monsieur,
items.en: Dear Sir or Madam,

items.hr: Cher Paul,
items.en: Dear Paul,

items.en: I would be grateful if you could reply to me.

items.hr: Je vous prie d'agréer mes salutations distinguées.
items.en: Yours faithfully. (formal closing)

items.en: Kisses, see you soon! (informal closing)

items.hr: N'hésitez pas à me contacter.
items.en: Do not hesitate to contact me.

activities.title: Formel ou informel ?
activities.intro: Practise choosing register-appropriate formulas.

questions.prompt: Which opening is formal, for an unknown recipient?
questions.explanation: 'Madame, Monsieur,' is the standard formal opening when you don't know the recipient.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct formal closing for a professional letter.
options: Je vous prie d'agréer mes salutations distinguées.
questions.answer: Je vous prie d'agréer mes salutations distinguées.
questions.explanation: This is a standard polite formula to end a formal letter.

questions.prompt: Complete the formal request: 'Je vous serais ___ de me répondre.'
questions.explanation: 'Je vous serais reconnaissant de' is a polite way to make a request.

questions.prompt: Type the subject pronoun you use with someone you address formally.
questions.explanation: 'Vous' is the formal (or plural) form of address.

questions.prompt: Which sentence belongs to an informal email?
options: Je vous prie de recevoir mes respects.
questions.explanation: 'Salut, comment ça va ?' is casual and used between friends.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Thank you for your quick reply.'
questions.explanation: 'Je vous remercie de' is a polite formal way to thank the recipient.

activities.title: Aider à écrire un mél
activities.intro: You play 'Me', helping a friend choose the right register for an email.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je dois écrire à mon professeur. Je commence par 'Salut' ?
lines.en: I have to write to my teacher. Do I start with 'Salut'?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, avec un professeur, il vaut mieux écrire 'Bonjour Monsieur'.
lines.en: No, with a teacher, it's better to write 'Bonjour Monsieur'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pour demander quelque chose poliment ?
lines.en: And to ask for something politely?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu peux écrire : 'Je vous serais reconnaissant de m'aider'.
lines.en: You can write: 'I would be grateful if you could help me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment je termine le message ?
lines.en: How do I end the message?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Termine par 'Cordialement' suivi de ton nom.
lines.en: End with 'Cordialement' followed by your name.

days.title: Le débat : accord et désaccord
days.objective: Take part in a debate expressing agreement and disagreement politely.
days.paretoFocus: Master phrases for agreeing, disagreeing, and conceding to structure a balanced argument.
drills: Write three ways to agree and three ways to disagree in French.
React to three opinions, alternating agreement and disagreement.
Practise a concession using 'certes... mais...' on two topics.

items: Recall three agreement and three disagreement phrases.
Make a concession with 'certes... mais'.
Politely disagree with one statement.

activities.title: Exprimer l'accord et le désaccord
activities.intro: Phrases to agree, disagree, and concede in a debate.

items.hr: Je suis tout à fait d'accord avec toi.
items.en: I completely agree with you.

items.hr: Tu as raison sur ce point.
items.en: You are right on this point.

items.hr: Je ne suis pas d'accord.
items.en: I don't agree.

items.hr: Je ne partage pas ton avis.
items.en: I don't share your opinion.

items.hr: Certes, c'est vrai, mais il faut aussi penser au coût.
items.en: Admittedly, that's true, but we must also think about the cost.

items.hr: Au contraire, je pense que c'est une erreur.
items.en: On the contrary, I think it's a mistake.

activities.title: Débattre en français
activities.intro: Practise agreement, disagreement, and concession structures.

questions.prompt: Which phrase expresses strong agreement?
options: Je suis tout à fait d'accord.
Je ne partage pas ton avis.
questions.answer: Je suis tout à fait d'accord.
questions.explanation: 'Tout à fait d'accord' expresses complete agreement.

questions.prompt: Choose the phrase that introduces a concession.
questions.explanation: 'Certes... mais' concedes a point before objecting to it.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite disagreement: 'Je ne ___ pas ton avis.'
questions.explanation: 'Je ne partage pas ton avis' politely expresses disagreement.

questions.prompt: Type the French phrase for 'on the contrary'.
questions.explanation: 'Au contraire' introduces an opposite viewpoint.

questions.prompt: Which sentence disagrees politely?
options: Je comprends ton point de vue, mais je ne suis pas d'accord.
questions.answer: Je comprends ton point de vue, mais je ne suis pas d'accord.
questions.explanation: It acknowledges the other view before disagreeing, which is polite.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I don't agree with this idea at all.'
questions.explanation: 'Ne... pas du tout d'accord' expresses total disagreement.

activities.title: Un débat sur les écrans
activities.intro: You play 'Me', debating screen time with a friend.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je pense que les enfants passent trop de temps devant les écrans.
lines.en: I think children spend too much time in front of screens.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je suis d'accord avec toi, c'est un vrai problème.
lines.en: I agree with you, it's a real problem.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il faudrait interdire les téléphones à l'école.
lines.en: We should ban phones at school.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certes, mais je pense qu'ils peuvent aussi être utiles pour apprendre.
lines.en: Admittedly, but I think they can also be useful for learning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Donc tu n'es pas d'accord ?
lines.en: So you don't agree?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je ne partage pas totalement ton avis : il faut surtout apprendre à les utiliser.
lines.en: I don't entirely share your opinion: we mainly need to learn how to use them.

days.title: Compréhension de l'oral : s'entraîner
days.objective: Build a listening strategy to identify the topic, key details and speaker intention in DELF B1 audio documents.
days.paretoFocus: Reading the questions BEFORE the audio starts guides your listening and captures most of the points.
drills: Listen to a 2-minute French radio clip and note who speaks, where, and why.
Predict answers from the questions before pressing play, then verify on the recording.
Write down 5 numbers/dates you hear and check spelling.

items: Recall: what should you do during the 30 seconds before each audio?
Re-listen to yesterday's clip and transcribe one sentence.
Say aloud three question words (qui, quand, pourquoi) and what each targets.

activities.title: Stratégies d'écoute
activities.intro: Core strategies to score well in the listening section.

items.hr: Lisez les questions avant l'écoute.
items.en: Read the questions before listening.

items.hr: Repérez le contexte : qui parle, où, pourquoi ?
items.en: Spot the context: who is speaking, where, why?

items.hr: Notez les mots-clés, pas des phrases entières.
items.en: Note key words, not whole sentences.

items.hr: Attention aux chiffres, dates et heures.
items.en: Pay attention to numbers, dates and times.

items.hr: Ne bloquez pas sur un mot inconnu, continuez.
items.en: Don't get stuck on an unknown word, keep going.

activities.title: Comprendre l'essentiel
activities.intro: Practise identifying purpose and detail.

questions.prompt: You hear: 'Le train à destination de Lyon partira à quai numéro 4.' What is announced?
questions.explanation: 'partira' (will leave) plus 'quai numéro 4' gives departure and platform.

questions.prompt: Which question word asks about a reason?
questions.explanation: 'Pourquoi' means 'why' and targets the reason.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'Read the questions before listening.'
questions.explanation: Imperative 'Lisez' + 'avant l'écoute' (before the listening).

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Il faut repérer le ____ : qui parle et pourquoi.' (context)
questions.explanation: 'le contexte' = the context.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Note down the important keywords.'
questions.explanation: Verb + article + noun + adjective (adjective follows the noun here).

activities.title: Consignes de l'examinateur
activities.intro: You play 'Me', the candidate receiving listening-test instructions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vous allez entendre deux fois un document sonore.
lines.en: You will hear an audio document twice.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord. Est-ce que je peux lire les questions d'abord ?
lines.en: Okay. May I read the questions first?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oui, vous avez trente secondes pour lire.
lines.en: Yes, you have thirty seconds to read.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Merci. Je vais souligner les mots importants.
lines.en: Thank you. I will underline the important words.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ensuite, vous répondrez aux questions.
lines.en: Then you will answer the questions.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Très bien, je suis prêt à écouter.
lines.en: Very well, I'm ready to listen.

days.title: Compréhension des écrits : s'entraîner
days.objective: Develop reading strategies to locate information quickly and understand a text's overall meaning.
days.paretoFocus: Skimming for the gist first, then scanning for specific details, answers most reading questions efficiently.
drills: Skim an article in 60 seconds and write its main idea in one sentence.
Scan a text to find three specific facts (a name, a place, a number).
Underline connectors (mais, donc, car) to follow the argument.

items: Recall: difference between skimming and scanning?
Re-read yesterday's text and summarise it in two sentences.
List three connectors and what each one signals.

activities.title: Stratégies de lecture
activities.intro: How to read efficiently under time pressure.

items.hr: Survolez le texte pour saisir l'idée générale.
items.en: Skim the text to grasp the general idea.

items.hr: Cherchez les informations précises demandées.
items.en: Scan for the specific information asked.

items.hr: Repérez les connecteurs logiques.
items.en: Spot the logical connectors.

items.hr: Le titre et les sous-titres donnent le thème.
items.en: The title and subtitles give the theme.

items.hr: Devinez le sens d'un mot grâce au contexte.
items.en: Guess a word's meaning from the context.

activities.title: Lire et repérer
activities.intro: Practise gist and detail reading.

questions.prompt: What does 'survoler un texte' mean as a reading strategy?
options: To translate it word by word
To skim it for the main idea
questions.answer: To skim it for the main idea
questions.explanation: 'Survoler' literally 'to fly over' = to skim for the gist.

questions.prompt: Which connector signals a consequence?
questions.explanation: 'donc' (therefore) introduces a consequence.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'the title gives the theme.'
questions.explanation: 'le titre' = the title, 'le thème' = the theme.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Je devine le sens grâce au ____.' (context)
questions.explanation: 'grâce au contexte' = thanks to the context.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Look for the specific information.'
questions.explanation: Verb + article + noun + adjective.

activities.title: Comprendre une consigne écrite
activities.intro: You play 'Me', the candidate discussing the reading task with the examiner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Lisez le texte, puis répondez aux questions.
lines.en: Read the text, then answer the questions.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dois-je lire le texte en entier d'abord ?
lines.en: Should I read the whole text first?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oui, survolez-le pour comprendre le sujet.
lines.en: Yes, skim it to understand the subject.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ensuite, je cherche les détails précis, c'est ça ?
lines.en: Then I look for the precise details, right?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Exactement. Justifiez vos réponses avec le texte.
lines.en: Exactly. Justify your answers with the text.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Compris, je vais souligner les passages utiles.
lines.en: Understood, I'll underline the useful passages.

days.title: Production écrite : l'essai d'opinion (160 mots)
days.objective: Learn to structure a 160-word opinion essay with a clear introduction, arguments and conclusion.
days.paretoFocus: A three-part plan (intro, two arguments, conclusion) with linking words earns most of the writing marks.
drills: Write a one-sentence introduction stating your opinion on a topic.
Draft two arguments, each with an example, using 'd'abord' and 'ensuite'.
Write a conclusion of one sentence beginning with 'En conclusion'.

items: Recall: the three parts of an opinion essay?
Rewrite one weak sentence adding a connector.
Count the words of a paragraph and adjust to target length.

activities.title: Structurer son essai
activities.intro: The building blocks of a B1 opinion essay.

items.hr: À mon avis, ... / Selon moi, ...
items.en: In my opinion, ...

items.hr: D'abord, ... Ensuite, ... Enfin, ...
items.en: First, ... Then, ... Finally, ...

items.hr: Par exemple, ...
items.en: For example, ...

items.hr: En conclusion, je pense que ...
items.en: In conclusion, I think that ...

items.hr: Il est important de donner son opinion clairement.
items.en: It is important to give your opinion clearly.

activities.title: Construire l'argumentation
activities.intro: Practise essay structure and phrasing.

questions.prompt: Which phrase best opens the conclusion of an essay?
questions.explanation: 'En conclusion' signals the closing paragraph.

questions.prompt: Which expression states a personal opinion?
questions.explanation: 'Selon moi' = 'in my opinion'.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'First, ... Then, ... Finally, ...'
questions.explanation: Standard sequence connectors for arguments.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Par ____, les transports en commun sont écologiques.' (example)
questions.explanation: 'Par exemple' = for example.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'In my opinion, this idea is interesting.'
questions.explanation: 'À mon avis' (in my opinion) opens the sentence, then subject + verb + adjective.

activities.title: Préparer le sujet d'essai
activities.intro: You play 'Me', the candidate clarifying the writing task.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Le sujet : faut-il interdire les voitures en ville ?
lines.en: The topic: should cars be banned in the city?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je dois écrire combien de mots environ ?
lines.en: About how many words should I write?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Environ cent soixante mots, avec votre opinion.
lines.en: About one hundred and sixty words, with your opinion.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je vais donner deux arguments et un exemple pour chacun.
lines.en: I'll give two arguments and one example for each.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Parfait. N'oubliez pas les connecteurs logiques.
lines.en: Perfect. Don't forget the logical connectors.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord, je terminerai par une conclusion claire.
lines.en: Okay, I'll end with a clear conclusion.

days.title: Production orale : le monologue suivi
days.objective: Learn to present and defend a viewpoint in a short structured monologue.
days.paretoFocus: Announcing your plan then giving one argument with an example makes the monologue clear and convincing.
drills: Speak for 90 seconds giving your opinion on screen time for children.
State a viewpoint, then justify it with 'parce que' and one example.
Record yourself and check you used at least three connectors.

items: Recall: how do you announce the structure of your monologue?
Rephrase one opinion using 'je suis convaincu(e) que'.
List three expressions to give examples.

activities.title: Présenter un point de vue
activities.intro: Phrases to structure and defend a viewpoint.

items.hr: Je vais vous parler de ...
items.en: I'm going to talk to you about ...

items.hr: Je suis convaincu(e) que ...
items.en: I am convinced that ...

items.hr: D'une part ..., d'autre part ...
items.en: On the one hand ..., on the other hand ...

items.hr: C'est pourquoi je pense que ...
items.en: That's why I think that ...

items.hr: Pour conclure, je dirais que ...
items.en: To conclude, I would say that ...

activities.title: Défendre son opinion
activities.intro: Practise viewpoint language.

questions.prompt: Which phrase introduces a conclusion in a monologue?
questions.explanation: 'Pour conclure' = 'to conclude'.

questions.prompt: Which expression shows a strong conviction?
questions.explanation: 'Je suis convaincu que' expresses certainty and conviction.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'On the one hand ..., on the other hand ...'
questions.explanation: Balanced connectors to present two sides.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'C'est ____ je pense que le sport est essentiel.' (that's why)
questions.explanation: 'C'est pourquoi' = that's why.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I'm going to tell you my opinion.'
questions.explanation: Near-future 'aller + infinitive' with the object pronoun 'vous' before 'parler'.

activities.title: Le monologue à l'examen
activities.intro: You play 'Me', the candidate presenting a monologue to the examiner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Choisissez un sujet et donnez votre point de vue.
lines.en: Choose a topic and give your point of view.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je vais vous parler des réseaux sociaux chez les jeunes.
lines.en: I'm going to talk about social media among young people.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Très bien, quels sont vos arguments ?
lines.en: Very good, what are your arguments?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'une part, ils aident à communiquer ; d'autre part, ils font perdre du temps.
lines.en: On the one hand, they help communication; on the other, they waste time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pouvez-vous donner un exemple concret ?
lines.en: Can you give a concrete example?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Par exemple, je passe deux heures par jour sur mon téléphone. Pour conclure, il faut un usage modéré.
lines.en: For example, I spend two hours a day on my phone. To conclude, moderate use is needed.

days.title: Production orale : l'interaction / le jeu de rôle
days.objective: Practise cooperative interaction and role-play to negotiate and reach an agreement.
days.paretoFocus: Asking questions and reacting to your partner keeps the interaction alive and scores interaction points.
drills: Role-play buying a train ticket and changing the date.
Practise polite disagreement using 'je comprends, mais ...'.
Ask three questions to keep a conversation going.

items: Recall: two polite ways to disagree in French?
Rephrase a demand as a polite request with the conditional.
List three expressions to propose a solution.

activities.title: Interagir et négocier
activities.intro: Phrases to cooperate and reach an agreement.

items.hr: Je propose que nous ...
items.en: I suggest that we ...

items.hr: Qu'est-ce que tu en penses ?
items.en: What do you think about it?

items.hr: Je comprends, mais je ne suis pas d'accord.
items.en: I understand, but I don't agree.

items.hr: On pourrait peut-être trouver un compromis.
items.en: We could maybe find a compromise.

items.hr: D'accord, on fait comme ça.
items.en: Okay, let's do it that way.

activities.title: Réagir et proposer
activities.intro: Practise interaction phrases.

questions.prompt: Which phrase politely expresses disagreement?
options: Je comprends, mais je ne suis pas d'accord.
Tu as tout à fait raison.
questions.answer: Je comprends, mais je ne suis pas d'accord.
questions.explanation: It acknowledges the other's view ('je comprends') before disagreeing politely.

questions.prompt: Which sentence proposes a compromise?
questions.explanation: Conditional 'pourrait' softens and proposes a middle ground.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'What do you think about it?' (informal)
questions.explanation: 'en' refers back to the thing being discussed.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Je ____ que nous partions plus tôt.' (suggest)
questions.explanation: 'Je propose que' + subjunctive ('partions').

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'We could find a compromise together.'
questions.explanation: Conditional 'pourrait' + infinitive 'trouver' + object + adverb.

activities.title: Jeu de rôle : organiser une sortie
activities.intro: You play 'Me', the candidate negotiating plans with the examiner playing a friend.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On pourrait aller au cinéma samedi soir ?
lines.en: Shall we go to the cinema on Saturday evening?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bonne idée, mais je préférerais l'après-midi.
lines.en: Good idea, but I would prefer the afternoon.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourquoi pas l'après-midi ? Qu'est-ce que tu proposes ?
lines.en: Why not the afternoon? What do you suggest?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je propose que nous nous retrouvions à quinze heures.
lines.en: I suggest that we meet at three o'clock.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ça me va. On invite aussi Léa ?
lines.en: That works for me. Shall we invite Léa too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord, on fait comme ça. Je l'appelle ce soir.
lines.en: Okay, let's do it that way. I'll call her tonight.

days.title: Les connecteurs logiques
days.objective: Master common logical connectors to link ideas of contrast, cause and consequence.
days.paretoFocus: Distinguishing cause (grâce à / à cause de) from consequence (donc) and contrast (cependant / pourtant) sharpens every argument.
drills: Join two clauses with 'cependant' to show contrast.
Write one positive cause with 'grâce à' and one negative cause with 'à cause de'.
Turn a cause-effect pair into a sentence using 'donc'.

items: Recall: difference between 'grâce à' and 'à cause de'?
Rewrite a sentence replacing 'mais' with 'pourtant'.
Give three connectors of consequence.

activities.title: Contraste, cause et conséquence
activities.intro: The meaning and use of key connectors.

items.hr: cependant / pourtant
items.en: however / yet

items.hr: grâce à
items.en: thanks to

items.hr: à cause de
items.en: because of

items.hr: donc
items.en: therefore / so

items.hr: Grâce à mes efforts, j'ai réussi ; à cause du bruit, j'ai mal dormi.
items.en: Thanks to my efforts I succeeded; because of the noise I slept badly.

activities.title: Choisir le bon connecteur
activities.intro: Practise connector meaning and use.

questions.prompt: Choose: 'Il a réussi ____ son travail.' (positive cause)
questions.explanation: 'grâce à' expresses a positive cause; the result (success) is good.

questions.prompt: Choose: 'Il pleut, ____ je prends un parapluie.' (consequence)
questions.explanation: 'donc' (therefore) introduces the logical consequence.

questions.prompt: Complete with a negative cause: 'Le train est annulé ____ la grève.'
questions.explanation: A cancelled train is negative, so 'à cause de' fits.

questions.prompt: Complete with a contrast connector: 'Il est fatigué, ____ il continue à travailler.'
questions.explanation: 'pourtant' (yet) marks the unexpected contrast.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Thanks to your help, I finished the project.'
questions.explanation: 'Grâce à' + cause, then the positive result in the passé composé.

activities.title: Expliquer avec des connecteurs
activities.intro: You play 'Me', the candidate explaining a situation using connectors.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourquoi êtes-vous arrivé en retard ?
lines.en: Why did you arrive late?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: À cause d'un embouteillage, j'ai raté le bus.
lines.en: Because of a traffic jam, I missed the bus.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et comment avez-vous fait ensuite ?
lines.en: And how did you manage afterwards?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Grâce à un collègue, j'ai pu venir en voiture.
lines.en: Thanks to a colleague, I was able to come by car.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vous auriez pu prévenir, non ?
lines.en: You could have warned us, couldn't you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mon téléphone était déchargé, donc je n'ai pas pu appeler ; cependant, je suis vraiment désolé.
lines.en: My phone was dead, so I couldn't call; however, I'm truly sorry.

days.title: Réviser le subjonctif et le conditionnel
days.objective: Review when to use the subjunctive and the conditional and form them correctly at B1 level.
days.paretoFocus: Triggers like 'il faut que' (subjunctive) and 'si + imparfait' (conditional) cover most B1 uses.
drills: Conjugate three verbs in the present subjunctive after 'il faut que'.
Write a polite request in the conditional with 'je voudrais'.
Complete two 'si' sentences: si + imparfait, then conditionnel.

items: Recall: three expressions that trigger the subjunctive?
Transform 'je veux' into a polite 'je voudrais' request.
State the tense pattern for a hypothesis with 'si'.

activities.title: Subjonctif et conditionnel
activities.intro: Key triggers and forms for both moods.

items.hr: Il faut que tu fasses tes devoirs.
items.en: You must do your homework.

items.hr: Je veux que tu sois à l'heure.
items.en: I want you to be on time.

items.hr: Je voudrais un café, s'il vous plaît.
items.en: I would like a coffee, please.

items.hr: Si j'avais le temps, je voyagerais.
items.en: If I had time, I would travel.

items.hr: Bien que ce soit difficile, je continue.
items.en: Although it is difficult, I continue.

activities.title: Subjonctif ou conditionnel ?
activities.intro: Practise choosing and forming the right mood.

questions.prompt: Choose: 'Il faut que tu ____ prudent.' (être)
questions.explanation: 'Il faut que' triggers the subjunctive: 'que tu sois'.

questions.prompt: Choose: 'Si j'avais de l'argent, je ____ une maison.' (acheter)
questions.explanation: si + imparfait requires the conditionnel présent 'achèterais'.

questions.prompt: Complete politely: 'Je ____ réserver une table.' (vouloir, conditional)
questions.explanation: 'Je voudrais' is the conditional of 'vouloir', used for polite requests.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Bien que ce ____ cher, j'achète ce livre.' (être, subjunctive)
questions.explanation: 'Bien que' triggers the subjunctive: 'que ce soit'.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'If you came, I would be glad.'
questions.explanation: si + imparfait 'venais', main clause in conditionnel 'serais'.

activities.title: Conseils et hypothèses
activities.intro: You play 'Me', the candidate using subjunctive and conditional with the examiner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que feriez-vous si vous gagniez au loto ?
lines.en: What would you do if you won the lottery?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si je gagnais, je voyagerais autour du monde.
lines.en: If I won, I would travel around the world.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pour vos études, que faut-il faire ?
lines.en: And for your studies, what must you do?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il faut que je travaille davantage et que je sois régulier.
lines.en: I must work more and be consistent.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Auriez-vous un conseil pour un débutant ?
lines.en: Would you have advice for a beginner?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je lui conseillerais de pratiquer chaque jour, bien que ce soit difficile.
lines.en: I would advise them to practise every day, although it is difficult.

days.title: Le futur antérieur : l'action finie avant une autre
days.objective: You can say that one future action will already be finished before another one happens.
days.paretoFocus: Planning, deadlines and promises all need this tense, and French uses it where English simply says 'have finished'.
drills: Write three sentences about tomorrow using 'quand j'aurai' plus a past participle.
Take five things on your to-do list and say by when each one will be done.
Say out loud what you will have achieved in five years, using three different verbs.

items: Which auxiliary do movement and reflexive verbs take in the futur antérieur?
How do you say 'as soon as she has arrived' in French?
Give one sentence where the futur antérieur guesses about a past event.

activities.title: Avant demain, tout sera fini
activities.intro: The futur antérieur is built with the simple future of avoir or être plus a past participle. It describes an action that will already be complete before another future moment. French uses it after quand, dès que, lorsque and une fois que, where English is happy with a present perfect.

items.hr: Quand j'aurai fini mon rapport, je t'appellerai.
items.en: When I have finished my report, I will call you.
items.note: avoir in the future plus the past participle

items.hr: Dès qu'elle sera arrivée, nous commencerons la réunion.
items.en: As soon as she has arrived, we will start the meeting.
items.note: arriver takes être, so the participle agrees

items.hr: Ils se seront couchés bien avant minuit.
items.en: They will have gone to bed well before midnight.

items.hr: Dans deux ans, j'aurai terminé mes études.
items.en: In two years I will have finished my studies.

items.hr: Tu auras sûrement oublié mon adresse.
items.en: You have probably forgotten my address.
items.note: the tense can also guess about something already done

activities.title: Former le futur antérieur
activities.intro: Practise building and choosing the futur antérieur.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Quand tu ___ tes valises, on partira.'
questions.explanation: Faire takes avoir, and the subject is tu, so the auxiliary is auras plus the participle fait.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses le futur antérieur correctly?
options: Elle sera partie avant ton retour.
Elle aura partie avant ton retour.
