activities.intro: In songs and casual speech the ne of the negation usually disappears and pronouns merge with the verb. Knowing the written form behind the sung one makes lyrics readable. A song alternates couplets and a refrain.

items.hr: Dans les chansons, je ne sais pas devient souvent j'sais pas.
items.en: In songs, je ne sais pas often becomes j'sais pas.
items.note: the vowel of je disappears in fast speech

items.hr: Le refrain revient après chaque couplet.
items.en: The chorus comes back after each verse.
items.note: le refrain = chorus, le couplet = verse

items.hr: À l'oral, le ne de la négation disparaît presque toujours.
items.en: In speech, the ne of the negation almost always disappears.
items.note: j'y crois pas = je n'y crois pas

items.hr: Les paroles jouent avec les rimes, donc l'ordre des mots peut surprendre.
items.en: Lyrics play with rhymes, so the word order can be surprising.

items.hr: Écoute d'abord le refrain, puis note les mots que tu reconnais.
items.en: Listen to the chorus first, then write down the words you recognise.

activities.title: Des paroles aux mots écrits
activities.intro: Practise turning sung shortcuts back into full written French.

questions.prompt: In sung and spoken French, 'j'y crois pas' is short for:
questions.explanation: The missing element is the ne of the negation, which comes before the pronoun y.

questions.prompt: What does 'le refrain' mean in a song?
questions.explanation: Le refrain is the part that comes back between verses, which are called les couplets.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Le ___ revient après chaque couplet.' (the part that repeats)
questions.explanation: Le refrain is masculine and names the repeated section of a song.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Écoute la chanson deux fois, ___ note les mots connus.' (then)
questions.explanation: Puis links two successive actions and is not followed by a comma here.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The lyrics play with rhymes.'
questions.explanation: Paroles is plural, so the verb is jouent, and jouer avec keeps its preposition.

activities.title: Tu comprends les paroles ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend is frustrated by song lyrics and you explain the shortened forms and how to listen.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je ne comprends rien aux paroles de cette chanson.
lines.en: I do not understand anything in the lyrics of this song.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est normal : à l'oral, le ne de la négation disparaît presque toujours.
lines.en: That is normal: in speech, the ne of the negation almost always disappears.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Elle chante j'sais pas, ça veut dire quoi ?
lines.en: She sings j'sais pas, what does that mean?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est simplement je ne sais pas, prononcé très vite.
lines.en: It is simply je ne sais pas, said very fast.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pourquoi l'ordre des mots est bizarre ?
lines.en: And why is the word order strange?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parce que les paroles jouent avec les rimes.
lines.en: Because lyrics play with rhymes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je devrais commencer par quoi, alors ?
lines.en: So what should I start with?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Écoute d'abord le refrain, puis note les mots que tu reconnais.
lines.en: Listen to the chorus first, then write down the words you recognise.

days.title: Le cinéma : raconter et critiquer un film
days.objective: You can summarise the plot of a film in the present tense and give a short opinion on it.
days.paretoFocus: Il s'agit de and trouver plus adjective are the two structures every French speaker uses to sum up and judge a story, book or film in one sentence.
drills: Summarise a film you know in two sentences, starting with il s'agit de.
Give three opinions using j'ai trouvé plus a noun plus an adjective.
Say whether the film is worth seeing, using ça vaut le coup de.

items: How do you say 'it is about a family' in French?
Which tense is used to tell the plot of a film?
Give the French for 'it is worth seeing'.

activities.title: Résumer et juger un film
activities.intro: Il s'agit de is impersonal: the subject il never changes and never names a person. Plots are told in the present tense. Trouver plus an object plus an adjective gives an opinion in one move.

items.hr: Il s'agit d'une famille qui déménage à la campagne.
items.en: It is about a family that moves to the countryside.
items.note: il s'agit de, impersonal, never il s'agit une

items.hr: Le film raconte l'histoire d'une jeune médecin.
items.en: The film tells the story of a young doctor.
items.note: raconter l'histoire de, present tense for plots

items.hr: J'ai trouvé la fin un peu prévisible.
items.en: I found the ending a bit predictable.

items.hr: Les acteurs jouent très juste, surtout le second rôle.
items.en: The actors act very convincingly, especially the supporting role.

items.hr: Ça vaut le coup d'aller le voir au cinéma.
items.en: It is worth going to see it at the cinema.
items.note: ça vaut le coup de + infinitive

activities.title: Une critique en trois phrases
activities.intro: Practise summarising a plot and stating an opinion.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says 'It is about a family'?
questions.explanation: The expression always keeps de after agit, and se elides to s' before the vowel.

questions.prompt: Which sentence gives an opinion on the ending naturally?
options: J'ai trouvé que la fin prévisible.
Je trouve la fin de prévisible.
J'ai trouvé la fin être prévisible.
questions.explanation: Trouver takes a direct object followed immediately by the adjective, with no que and no verb between them.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Ce film ___ le coup, vas-y ce week-end !' (it is worth it)
questions.explanation: Valoir le coup means to be worth it, and the third person singular is vaut.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Le film ___ l'histoire de deux frères.' (tells)
questions.explanation: Raconter is a regular -er verb and plots are told in the present tense.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The actors act very convincingly.'
questions.explanation: Juste works as an adverb after jouer and stays invariable.

activities.title: À la sortie du cinéma
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You have just seen a film and a friend asks about it, so you summarise it and give your opinion.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, ce film, ça parle de quoi ?
lines.en: So, what is this film about?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il s'agit d'une famille qui déménage à la campagne et qui recommence tout.
lines.en: It is about a family that moves to the countryside and starts everything again.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et c'est bien joué ?
lines.en: And is it well acted?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Les acteurs jouent très juste, surtout le second rôle.
lines.en: The actors act very convincingly, especially the supporting role.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il y a un point qui t'a déçu ?
lines.en: Is there anything that disappointed you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai trouvé la fin un peu prévisible, mais ça reste agréable.
lines.en: I found the ending a bit predictable, but it is still enjoyable.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu me conseilles d'y aller ?
lines.en: Do you advise me to go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, ça vaut le coup de le voir sur grand écran.
lines.en: Yes, it is worth seeing it on a big screen.

days.title: Lire un court récit : stratégies de lecture
days.objective: You can recognise written narrative past forms in a short story and use reading strategies instead of translating every word.
days.paretoFocus: Written stories use a past tense you never hear in conversation, so recognising it turns whole pages of French fiction from impossible into readable.
drills: Convert three written past forms into the spoken past you already know.
Read any short French text once without a dictionary and note the main events.
Underline the verbs in a paragraph and put the events in order.

items: Which spoken form corresponds to 'il entra'?
What should you do before looking up an unknown word?
Give the French verb meaning 'to guess'.

activities.title: Lire une histoire courte
activities.intro: Written stories use a narrative past that is not used in speech: il entra corresponds to il est entré. You only need to recognise it, not produce it. Reading whole paragraphs first and guessing from context is faster than looking up every word.

items.hr: Dans les récits écrits, il est entré devient souvent il entra.
items.en: In written stories, il est entré often becomes il entra.

items.hr: Elle prit son sac et sortit sans rien dire.
items.en: She took her bag and went out without saying anything.
items.note: prit = a pris, sortit = est sortie

items.hr: Ne cherche pas chaque mot : lis d'abord le paragraphe entier.
items.en: Do not look up every word: read the whole paragraph first.
items.note: chercher un mot = to look a word up

items.hr: Le contexte aide souvent à deviner le sens d'un mot inconnu.
items.en: Context often helps to guess the meaning of an unknown word.

items.hr: Souligne les verbes pour suivre l'ordre des événements.
items.en: Underline the verbs to follow the order of the events.

activities.title: Reconnaître le passé du récit
activities.intro: Practise matching written narrative forms to the spoken past and applying reading strategies.

questions.prompt: 'Il entra dans la pièce' corresponds in everyday speech to:
options: il est entré dans la pièce
questions.answer: il est entré dans la pièce
questions.explanation: The written narrative past describes a single completed action, which is expressed with the spoken compound past.

questions.prompt: 'Elle prit son sac' is the written narrative form of:
questions.explanation: Prit is a single completed action in the past, matching a pris rather than the background form prenait.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Le contexte aide à ___ le sens d'un mot inconnu.' (to work out without a dictionary)
questions.explanation: Aider à is followed by an infinitive, and deviner means to guess.

questions.prompt: Complete the spoken equivalent with one word: 'elle sortit' = 'elle est ___'.
questions.explanation: Sortir takes être in the compound past, so the participle agrees with the feminine subject.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Underline the verbs to follow the order of the events.'
questions.explanation: The tu imperative of an -er verb has no final s, and pour plus an infinitive expresses purpose.

activities.title: Un livre trop difficile ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend is stuck on a short story in French and you explain the narrative past and reading strategies.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je bloque sur cette nouvelle, les verbes sont bizarres.
lines.en: I am stuck on this short story, the verbs are strange.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu as vu il entra ? C'est simplement il est entré, à l'écrit.
lines.en: Have you seen il entra? It is simply il est entré, in writing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et elle prit, alors ?
lines.en: And elle prit, then?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ça correspond à elle a pris. Tu n'as pas besoin de l'utiliser toi-même.
lines.en: That corresponds to elle a pris. You do not need to use it yourself.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je cherche chaque mot dans le dictionnaire et j'avance très lentement.
lines.en: I look up every word in the dictionary and I go very slowly.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne cherche pas chaque mot : lis d'abord le paragraphe entier.
lines.en: Do not look up every word: read the whole paragraph first.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et si je ne comprends toujours pas ?
lines.en: And if I still do not understand?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Souligne les verbes : le contexte aide souvent à deviner le reste.
lines.en: Underline the verbs: context often helps to guess the rest.

days.title: Exprimer un souhait, un regret et une intention
days.objective: You can express a wish, a regret about the past and a concrete plan, each with its own structure.
days.paretoFocus: The difference between j'aimerais and j'aurais aimé is the difference between a wish and a regret, and it is one of the clearest signs of control at this level.
drills: Say three wishes about your future with j'aimerais.
Say two regrets about your past with j'aurais aimé.
Write two concrete plans using avoir l'intention de and compter.

items: Which form expresses a regret about the past?
Which preposition follows regretter before an infinitive?
Give the French for 'I plan to leave in September'.

activities.title: Souhaits, regrets et projets
activities.intro: The conditional aimerais expresses a present wish, and the past conditional aurais aimé expresses a regret about something that did not happen. Regretter de is followed by a past infinitive. Avoir l'intention de and compter announce a plan.

items.hr: J'aimerais vivre un an à l'étranger.
items.en: I would like to live abroad for a year.

items.hr: J'aurais aimé apprendre le piano quand j'étais petit.
items.en: I would have liked to learn the piano when I was little.
items.note: past conditional = regret about the past

items.hr: Je regrette de ne pas avoir dit la vérité.
items.en: I regret not having told the truth.
items.note: regretter de + past infinitive, ne pas before avoir

items.hr: J'ai l'intention de reprendre mes études l'année prochaine.
items.en: I intend to go back to studying next year.

items.hr: Je compte partir en septembre, si tout va bien.
items.en: I plan to leave in September, if all goes well.

activities.title: Ce que je voudrais, ce que je regrette
activities.intro: Practise separating wishes, regrets and plans.

questions.prompt: Which sentence expresses a regret about the past?
questions.explanation: The past conditional aurais aimé refers to something that did not happen, which is a regret.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct completion: 'Je regrette ___ ne pas avoir répondu.'
questions.explanation: Regretter takes de before an infinitive, and the negation ne pas stays together before avoir.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'J'ai l'___ de déménager au printemps.' (a firm plan)
questions.explanation: Avoir l'intention de plus an infinitive announces a decided plan.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Je ___ partir en septembre.' (I plan to, verb used directly with an infinitive)
questions.explanation: Compter plus a bare infinitive means to plan to do something, with no preposition.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I would have liked to learn the piano.'
questions.explanation: The past conditional is formed with the conditional of avoir plus the participle aimé, followed by the infinitive.

activities.title: Projets et regrets
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks about your plans, and you answer with a wish, a regret and a firm intention.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as des projets pour l'année prochaine ?
lines.en: Do you have plans for next year?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'aimerais vivre un an à l'étranger.
lines.en: I would like to live abroad for a year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu ne l'as jamais fait avant ?
lines.en: You have never done it before?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, et j'aurais aimé partir juste après mes études.
lines.en: No, and I would have liked to leave right after my studies.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourquoi tu ne l'as pas fait ?
lines.en: Why did you not do it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je regrette de ne pas avoir osé à l'époque.
lines.en: I regret not having dared at the time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et cette fois, c'est décidé ?
lines.en: And this time, is it decided?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je compte partir en septembre et j'ai l'intention de tout organiser cet été.
lines.en: Yes, I plan to leave in September and I intend to organise everything this summer.

days.title: Nuancer son opinion : atténuer et insister
days.objective: You can soften an opinion to sound diplomatic, or strengthen it when you want to be firm.
days.paretoFocus: Softening and strengthening are what separate a blunt learner from a credible speaker: the same opinion sounds rude or reasonable depending on three or four small words.
drills: Take one strong opinion and soften it twice with different expressions.
Say two sentences that concede a point first and then object.
Write three opinions and mark each one as soft or firm.

items: Which expression sounds softer than 'je pense que'?
How do you concede a point before objecting, in French?
Give a French phrase that adds force to an opinion.

activities.title: Adoucir ou renforcer une opinion
activities.intro: Il me semble que sounds less direct than je pense que and still takes the indicative. Plutôt weakens an adjective, while franchement and je dirais même que strengthen a claim. Certes plus mais concedes a point before objecting.

items.hr: Il me semble que cette solution coûte trop cher.
items.en: It seems to me that this solution costs too much.
items.note: softer than je pense que, still indicative

items.hr: C'est plutôt une bonne idée, mais je resterais prudent.
items.en: It is quite a good idea, but I would stay careful.

items.hr: Franchement, je ne suis pas du tout d'accord.
items.en: Frankly, I do not agree at all.

items.hr: Certes, le prix est élevé, mais la qualité suit.
items.en: Admittedly, the price is high, but the quality matches it.

items.hr: Je dirais même que c'est la meilleure option.
items.en: I would even say that it is the best option.
items.note: je dirais même que pushes the point further

activities.title: Plus doux, plus fort
activities.intro: Practise choosing between softening and strengthening an opinion.

questions.prompt: Which expression makes an opinion sound softer?
questions.explanation: Il me semble que presents the opinion as a personal impression, so it sounds less categorical.

questions.prompt: Choose the link word: 'Certes, c'est cher, ___ ça dure longtemps.'
questions.explanation: Certes concedes a point and the objection that follows is introduced by mais.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'C'est ___ une bonne idée, mais il faut y réfléchir.' (softener meaning rather)
questions.explanation: Placed before the article, plutôt makes the judgement less absolute.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: '___, je ne suis pas d'accord du tout.' (opening adverb meaning honestly)
questions.explanation: Franchement at the start of the sentence announces a direct and firm opinion.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I would even say that it is the best option.'
questions.explanation: Même follows the conditional verb dirais and reinforces what comes after que.

activities.title: Une réunion tendue
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague defends a costly plan and you react, first softening your opinion and then insisting.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je propose qu'on change tout le matériel cette année.
lines.en: I suggest we change all the equipment this year.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il me semble que cette solution coûte trop cher.
lines.en: It seems to me that this solution costs too much.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Le matériel actuel est vieux, tu le sais bien.
lines.en: The current equipment is old, you know that well.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certes, il est vieux, mais il fonctionne encore correctement.
lines.en: Admittedly, it is old, but it still works properly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Donc tu es contre le projet ?
lines.en: So you are against the project?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est plutôt une bonne idée sur le fond, mais je resterais prudent sur le budget.
lines.en: It is quite a good idea in principle, but I would stay careful about the budget.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et si on remplaçait seulement la moitié ?
lines.en: And what if we replaced only half of it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Là, je dirais même que c'est la meilleure option.
lines.en: In that case, I would even say that it is the best option.

days.title: Structurer un exposé oral de trois minutes
days.objective: You can announce your topic, move through two or three parts and close a short talk cleanly.
days.paretoFocus: A talk that is clearly signposted sounds far more competent than one with better vocabulary and no structure, and the signposting phrases are only five expressions long.
drills: Write the opening sentence of a three minute talk on a topic you know.
Say aloud the four signposting phrases in order, without notes.
Give a one minute talk with an opening, two parts and a closing sentence.

items: Which phrase opens the first part of a talk?
Give a French phrase meaning 'moreover'.
How do you announce your topic at the very start?

activities.title: Annoncer, développer, conclure
activities.intro: A short talk needs four signposts: announcing the topic, opening the first part, adding a point and closing. The simple future is common in the announcement of the plan. Par ailleurs adds a new argument rather than opposing one.

items.hr: Aujourd'hui, je vais vous parler du travail à distance.
items.en: Today I am going to talk to you about remote work.
items.note: je vais vous parler de = standard opener

items.hr: Tout d'abord, je présenterai les avantages.
items.en: First of all, I will present the advantages.
items.note: tout d'abord + simple future for the plan

items.hr: Ensuite, j'aborderai deux problèmes concrets.
items.en: Then I will deal with two concrete problems.
items.note: aborder un point = to tackle a point

items.hr: Par ailleurs, il faut penser aux personnes isolées.
items.en: Moreover, we have to think about isolated people.

items.hr: Pour conclure, je dirais que l'équilibre reste possible.
items.en: To conclude, I would say that balance remains possible.

activities.title: Les mots qui guident l'auditeur
activities.intro: Practise the signposting phrases of a short spoken presentation.

questions.prompt: Which phrase opens the first part of a talk?
questions.explanation: Tout d'abord announces the first point; en revanche introduces a contrast, not a first step.

questions.prompt: What does 'par ailleurs' mean?
questions.explanation: Par ailleurs adds another argument on the same side; a contrast would need en revanche or pourtant.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Pour ___, je dirais que le sujet reste ouvert.' (to close the talk)
questions.explanation: Pour plus an infinitive expresses purpose, and conclure is the verb used for closing a presentation.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Ensuite, j'___ la question du coût.' (I will tackle, simple future of the verb meaning to take up a point)
questions.explanation: The simple future is built on the infinitive plus -ai for the je form.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Today I am going to talk to you about remote work.'
questions.explanation: The object pronoun vous goes before the infinitive, and parler de plus le contracts to du.

activities.title: Répéter son exposé
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A classmate listens to your talk plan and you use the signposting phrases to present it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vas-y, commence, je t'écoute.
lines.en: Go ahead, start, I am listening.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Aujourd'hui, je vais vous parler du travail à distance.
lines.en: Today I am going to talk to you about remote work.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: D'accord, et tu organises ça comment ?
lines.en: All right, and how are you organising it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tout d'abord, je présenterai les avantages, ensuite j'aborderai deux problèmes concrets.
lines.en: First of all, I will present the advantages, then I will deal with two concrete problems.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as un argument auquel personne ne pense ?
lines.en: Do you have an argument that nobody thinks of?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, par ailleurs il faut penser aux personnes isolées.
lines.en: Yes, moreover we have to think about isolated people.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et tu termines sur quoi ?
lines.en: And what do you finish on?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pour conclure, je dirais que l'équilibre reste possible.
lines.en: To conclude, I would say that balance remains possible.

days.title: Compréhension de l'oral : repérer l'information clé
days.objective: You can prepare for a listening test by reading the questions first and catching the key facts of a recording.
days.paretoFocus: In a listening exam most points are lost before the audio even starts, so the habit of reading the questions and knowing what to listen for is worth more than extra vocabulary.
drills: Before any listening practice, read the questions and underline the question words.
Listen to a short recording and write only who is speaking, to whom and why.
Practise noting numbers and times in French as you hear them, without full sentences.

items: What should you do in the pause before the recording starts?
What does the question 'À qui s'adresse ce message ?' ask about?
Name two kinds of detail that are almost always tested.

activities.title: Écouter pour répondre
activities.intro: Listening tasks reward preparation more than perfect understanding. Read the questions in the pause, identify the speaker, the audience and the purpose, and note figures fast. Missing a word is normal, and the general meaning is usually enough.

items.hr: Avant l'écoute, lis les questions et souligne les mots interrogatifs.
items.en: Before listening, read the questions and underline the question words.
items.note: mots interrogatifs: qui, quand, combien, pourquoi

items.hr: Repère d'abord qui parle et pourquoi.
items.en: First spot who is speaking and why.

items.hr: Les chiffres et les heures reviennent souvent : note-les tout de suite.
items.en: Figures and times come up often: write them down straight away.

items.hr: Une annonce publique commence souvent par mesdames et messieurs.
items.en: A public announcement often begins with ladies and gentlemen.
items.note: the opening tells you the audience

items.hr: Si tu perds un mot, continue : le sens général suffit souvent.
items.en: If you lose a word, keep going: the general meaning is often enough.

activities.title: Stratégies d'écoute
activities.intro: Practise the reflexes that save points in a listening test.

questions.prompt: In a listening test, what should you do during the pause before the recording?
options: Translate the questions word by word
questions.explanation: Reading the questions first tells you exactly which information to listen for.

questions.prompt: The question 'À qui s'adresse ce message ?' asks about:
questions.explanation: S'adresser à means to be aimed at someone, so the question asks who the message is for.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Note vite les ___ et les heures pendant l'écoute.' (numerical details)
questions.explanation: Les chiffres covers figures and amounts, which are the details most often tested.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Avant l'écoute, ___ attentivement les questions.' (read, tu imperative)
questions.explanation: The tu imperative of lire is lis, without the subject pronoun.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'First spot who is speaking and why.'
questions.explanation: The imperative comes first, then the adverb, then the two indirect questions joined by et.

activities.title: Avant l'épreuve d'écoute
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A classmate panics about the listening paper and you share your strategy for spotting key information.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai peur de l'épreuve d'écoute, je ne comprends jamais tout.
lines.en: I am afraid of the listening paper, I never understand everything.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ce n'est pas grave : si tu perds un mot, continue, le sens général suffit souvent.
lines.en: That is not a problem: if you lose a word, keep going, the general meaning is often enough.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pendant la pause, on fait quoi ?
lines.en: And during the pause, what do we do?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Lis les questions et souligne les mots interrogatifs.
lines.en: Read the questions and underline the question words.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je dois écrire pendant que j'écoute ?
lines.en: Should I write while I listen?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Seulement les chiffres et les heures : note-les tout de suite.
lines.en: Only figures and times: write them down straight away.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pour la première question, souvent c'est quoi ?
lines.en: And for the first question, what is it usually?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Repère d'abord qui parle et pourquoi, c'est presque toujours demandé.
lines.en: First spot who is speaking and why, that is almost always asked.

days.title: Production écrite : le mél argumenté
days.objective: You can write a formal email that states a problem, justifies it with arguments and asks for a concrete answer.
days.paretoFocus: The opening line, the argument connectors and the closing formula are fixed blocks: learn them once and the only thing left to invent is your actual argument.
drills: Write the opening and closing lines of a formal email from memory.
Draft three arguments about a local problem, each introduced by a different connector.
Write a full email of about eighty words asking an organisation to change something.

items: How do you address an organisation when you do not know the person's name?
Which connector introduces the justification of your point?
Give the standard closing formula of a formal email.

activities.title: Écrire un message argumenté
activities.intro: A formal email has fixed blocks: a neutral opening, the reason for writing, arguments, a request and a set closing formula. En effet justifies what you have just claimed, while c'est pourquoi introduces the consequence you draw from it.

items.hr: Madame, Monsieur, je me permets de vous écrire au sujet du service de bus.
items.en: Dear Sir or Madam, I am writing to you about the bus service.
items.note: je me permets de + infinitive, polite opener

items.hr: En effet, les horaires du soir ne conviennent plus aux habitants.
items.en: Indeed, the evening timetable no longer suits residents.

items.hr: De plus, l'arrêt le plus proche se trouve à vingt minutes à pied.
items.en: Moreover, the nearest stop is twenty minutes away on foot.
items.note: de plus adds a second argument

items.hr: C'est pourquoi je vous demande d'étudier une nouvelle solution.
items.en: That is why I am asking you to look at a new solution.

items.hr: Dans l'attente de votre réponse, je vous prie d'agréer mes salutations distinguées.
items.en: Looking forward to your reply, please accept my best regards.
items.note: standard formal closing, learn it as one block

activities.title: Les blocs du courrier formel
activities.intro: Practise the fixed openings, connectors and closing of a formal email.

questions.prompt: Which opening suits a formal email to an organisation you do not know?
questions.explanation: Madame, Monsieur is the neutral formula when you do not know who will read the message.

questions.prompt: Which connector introduces the consequence you draw from your arguments?
questions.explanation: En effet justifies a claim, whereas c'est pourquoi announces the conclusion and usually the request.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Je me ___ de vous écrire au sujet des horaires.' (polite verb of the opening line)
questions.explanation: Se permettre de plus an infinitive is the polite way to announce why you are writing.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Dans l'___ de votre réponse, je vous prie d'agréer mes salutations distinguées.' (while waiting for)
questions.explanation: Dans l'attente de is the fixed phrase that opens the closing sentence of a formal message.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Moreover, the nearest stop is twenty minutes away on foot.'
questions.explanation: The superlative le plus proche follows the noun, and se trouver is the usual verb for location.

activities.title: Préparer le message ensemble
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour wants to complain about the bus service and you dictate the structure of a formal email.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je commence comment ? Bonjour, ça suffit ?
lines.en: How do I start? Is bonjour enough?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, écris Madame, Monsieur, puis je me permets de vous écrire au sujet du service de bus.
lines.en: No, write Madame, Monsieur, then I am writing to you about the bus service.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ensuite j'explique le problème ?
lines.en: Then I explain the problem?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui : en effet, les horaires du soir ne conviennent plus aux habitants.
lines.en: Yes: indeed, the evening timetable no longer suits residents.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai un deuxième argument, l'arrêt est très loin.
lines.en: I have a second argument, the stop is very far.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Écris de plus, l'arrêt le plus proche se trouve à vingt minutes à pied.
lines.en: Write moreover, the nearest stop is twenty minutes away on foot.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et je demande quoi, exactement ?
lines.en: And what exactly do I ask for?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est pourquoi je vous demande d'étudier une nouvelle solution, puis tu fermes avec dans l'attente de votre réponse.
lines.en: That is why I am asking you to look at a new solution, then you close with looking forward to your reply.

days.title: S'entraîner à l'oral : simulation guidée
days.objective: You can keep a guided speaking test going by asking for repetition, buying time, correcting yourself and closing your turn.
days.paretoFocus: Silence and panic cost more marks than mistakes, so the small phrases that keep you talking while you think are the highest value thing to automate before a speaking test.
drills: Say the repetition request aloud five times until it comes out without hesitation.
Answer three random questions, starting each answer with a time buying phrase.
Record yourself speaking for one minute and end with a clear closing sentence.

items: How do you politely ask for a question to be repeated?
Which phrase checks that you understood the question correctly?
Give a short phrase that signals you have finished your answer.

activities.title: Gérer un entretien oral
activities.intro: In a guided speaking test you are marked on interaction, not only on accuracy. Asking for repetition, reformulating the question and using short fillers all show control. A clear closing sentence tells the examiner your turn is over.

items.hr: Pardon, pouvez-vous répéter la question, s'il vous plaît ?
items.en: Sorry, could you repeat the question, please?

items.hr: Alors, comment dire... je pense surtout à deux choses.
items.en: Well, how can I put it... I am mainly thinking of two things.
items.note: comment dire buys a second of thinking time

items.hr: Enfin, je veux dire que le prix n'est pas le seul critère.
items.en: Well, I mean that price is not the only criterion.
items.note: je veux dire introduces a self-correction

items.hr: Si j'ai bien compris, vous me demandez mon avis personnel.
items.en: If I have understood correctly, you are asking for my personal opinion.

items.hr: Voilà, c'est tout ce que je voulais dire.
items.en: There we are, that is all I wanted to say.

activities.title: Rester à l'aise à l'oral
activities.intro: Practise the phrases that keep a spoken exchange going under pressure.

questions.prompt: Which sentence politely asks for a repetition?
questions.explanation: The question form with pouvoir plus s'il vous plaît is polite; the bare imperative sounds like an order.

questions.prompt: The phrase 'Si j'ai bien compris, ...' is used to:
questions.explanation: It reformulates the question so the other person can confirm or correct you.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Alors, comment ___... laissez-moi réfléchir une seconde.' (verb meaning to say)
questions.explanation: Comment dire is a fixed filler with the infinitive, used while you look for your words.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: '___, c'est tout ce que je voulais dire.' (closing word meaning there we are)
questions.explanation: Voilà signals that you have finished your turn and hands the floor back.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'If I have understood correctly, you want my personal opinion.'
questions.explanation: Bien sits between the auxiliary and the participle, and the adjective personnel follows the noun.

activities.title: Simulation d'un oral
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner acts as the examiner in a practice speaking test and you use the interaction phrases to stay in control.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonjour. Pensez-vous que les transports gratuits soient une bonne idée ?
lines.en: Hello. Do you think free public transport is a good idea?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pardon, pouvez-vous répéter la question, s'il vous plaît ?
lines.en: Sorry, could you repeat the question, please?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bien sûr. Les transports gratuits, pour ou contre ?
lines.en: Of course. Free public transport, for or against?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si j'ai bien compris, vous me demandez mon avis personnel.
lines.en: If I have understood correctly, you are asking for my personal opinion.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Exactement. Je vous écoute.
lines.en: Exactly. I am listening.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Alors, comment dire... je pense surtout à deux choses : le coût et la fréquentation.
lines.en: Well, how can I put it... I am mainly thinking of two things: the cost and the number of users.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Le coût vous inquiète, donc ?
lines.en: So the cost worries you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Enfin, je veux dire que le prix n'est pas le seul critère. Voilà, c'est tout ce que je voulais dire.
lines.en: Well, I mean that price is not the only criterion. There we are, that is all I wanted to say.

days.title: Bilan B1 : mini-examen blanc
days.objective: Consolidate all four DELF B1 skills through a short mock exam and review of key strategies.
days.paretoFocus: Rehearsing the timing and structure of each section reduces stress and prevents avoidable mistakes on exam day.
drills: Do one mini task from each of the four sections back to back.
Time a 10-minute writing task and count your words.
Record a one-minute monologue and list two things to improve.

items: Recall: the four sections of the DELF B1 exam?
Name one strategy learned for listening and one for writing.
List three connectors you will reuse in the oral exam.

activities.title: Réviser les quatre épreuves
activities.intro: A recap of the four DELF B1 sections and one tip each.

items.hr: Compréhension de l'oral : lire les questions avant.
items.en: Listening: read the questions beforehand.

items.hr: Compréhension des écrits : survoler puis chercher les détails.
items.en: Reading: skim then look for details.

items.hr: Production écrite : plan clair et connecteurs.
items.en: Writing: clear plan and connectors.

items.hr: Production orale : donner son opinion et la justifier.
items.en: Speaking: give your opinion and justify it.

items.hr: Gérez votre temps dans chaque épreuve.
items.en: Manage your time in each section.

activities.title: Mini-examen blanc
activities.intro: Mixed questions covering all four skills.

questions.prompt: Which section requires you to write an opinion essay of about 160 words?
questions.explanation: The opinion essay belongs to the written production section.

questions.prompt: In the speaking exam, which connector helps you conclude?
questions.explanation: 'Pour conclure' signals the closing of your argument.

questions.prompt: Type the French name of the reading section of the exam.
questions.explanation: 'Compréhension des écrits' = reading comprehension.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Il faut ____ son temps dans chaque épreuve.' (manage)
questions.explanation: 'gérer son temps' = to manage one's time.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I have to manage my time.'
questions.explanation: 'devoir' + infinitive 'gérer' + object 'mon temps'.

activities.title: Débriefing de l'examen blanc
activities.intro: You play 'Me', the candidate reviewing the mock exam with the examiner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment avez-vous trouvé l'examen blanc ?
lines.en: How did you find the mock exam?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: L'oral était difficile, mais j'ai réussi à donner mon opinion.
lines.en: The oral was hard, but I managed to give my opinion.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce que vous devez améliorer ?
lines.en: What do you need to improve?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il faut que je gère mieux mon temps à l'écrit.
lines.en: I must manage my time better in writing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pour la compréhension de l'oral ?
lines.en: And for listening comprehension?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Grâce aux stratégies, je repère mieux les mots-clés ; donc je suis plus confiant.
lines.en: Thanks to the strategies, I spot key words better; so I'm more confident.
